page: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a member of the DDCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 24 physical cores and 24 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. Cache resources consist of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and cryptographic accelerations like AES, VAES, and SHA-NI.

The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 1843 GB of local disk space, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile positions the server as a memory-optimized and storage-capable option, offering a balance of high-capacity RAM and substantial local storage.

Benchmark evaluations show strong multi-core and throughput capabilities. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and an image processing score of 15819.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 43.73 GB/sec. For small-scale language model tasks, the server demonstrates strong performance, processing 2326.72 tokens/sec for prompts and 296.4 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. However, performance scales down on larger models, with a 70B model yielding a weak text generation speed of 2.74 tokens/sec. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks, such as Passmark (41779.09 CPU score) and Redis SET operations (45.93 million operations/sec), fall within the average range.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core and bundled 1843 GB local storage, the DC24ds_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of external storage attachments. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its strong multi-core performance and web serving throughput make it highly suitable for database operations, memory-intensive applications, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is an x86_64 server in the DDCSv3 family, powered by a dedicated 24-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 1843 GB of local storage. The server lacks GPU accelerators and public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in multi-core processing, static web serving (43.73 GB/sec), and small-model LLM inference, while standard database and cryptographic operations perform in the average range. This hardware profile offers qualitative resource density for memory-heavy applications. It is well-suited for database hosting, memory-intensive enterprise workloads, static web serving, and moderate language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a 24-vCPU server with 192 GB RAM and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized, storage-equipped server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores for demanding database, web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
1843 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong static web serving throughput of 43.73 GB/sec
Strong multi-core performance in image processing and small LLM inference
Best suited for databases, memory-intensive applications, and web serving
